title: session-consistent-lifecycle-gatekeeper-for-experiments-and-canaries
preview:
Context and motivation:
This PR introduces the 'Lifecycle Gatekeeper', a coordinator that unifies canary deploys, A/B experiments, shadow seeding, and automated rollback workflows while preserving session consistency for stateful flows (chat sessions, transactions, etc.). Prod teams requested an out-of-the-box mecha...
text:
Context and motivation:
This PR introduces the 'Lifecycle Gatekeeper', a coordinator that unifies canary deploys, A/B experiments, shadow seeding, and automated rollback workflows while preserving session consistency for stateful flows (chat sessions, transactions, etc.). Prod teams requested an out-of-the-box mechanism that: 1) keeps user sessions routed to stable variants during phased rollouts, 2) applies SLO/QA gates driven by telemetry windows, and 3) supports staged shadow traffic with backpressure and replay for testing.

Design summary:
- Lease-based session bonding: the gatekeeper issues short-lived leases tied to session identifiers (cookie/session_id). During ramp phases, existing sessions remain bound to the pre-rollout variant while new sessions may be admitted to target variants per policy. Leases survive process restarts and are persisted in the existing session-store a...

title: Hybrid comm/compute: staggered NCCL streams + dynamic kernel placement for tensor-parallel workloads
preview: Motivation: For TP models we observed tail latency spikes when large collective operations serialized across the default NCCL stream and blocked compute kernels on subsequent micro-batches. This PR implements a hybrid comm/compute execution strategy: staggered NCCL comm streams per tensor-parallel group, opportunist...
text:
Motivation: For TP models we observed tail latency spikes when large collective operations serialized across the default NCCL stream and blocked compute kernels on subsequent micro-batches. This PR implements a hybrid comm/compute execution strategy: staggered NCCL comm streams per tensor-parallel group, opportunistic kernel placement on secondary compute streams, and a lightweight dynamic placement heuristic that routes small-kernel work to a dedicated low-latency stream to maximize overlap without increasing memory pressure.

Summary of changes: implemented a per-TP-group stream allocator that staggers all-reduce/all-gather launches across NCCL streams (comm_stream_0..N) to reduce contention; added a kernel placement layer in the runtime scheduler that classifies kernels by inferred latency (fast vs slow) and assigns them to either the main compute stream or a fast-path microbatch s...

title: introduce-cost-caddy-prefill-heuristics-and-quant-fallbacks
preview: Motivation: Many tenants ship with conservative prefill and quantization defaults which are safe but expensive. This PR introduces 'Cost Caddy', a lightweight prefill-heuristics controller that applies tenant-aware default batching/caching presets and a safe quantization fallback policy to reduce unit token cost whi...
text:
Motivation: Many tenants ship with conservative prefill and quantization defaults which are safe but expensive. This PR introduces 'Cost Caddy', a lightweight prefill-heuristics controller that applies tenant-aware default batching/caching presets and a safe quantization fallback policy to reduce unit token cost while preserving quality SLOs. Goals: 1) Provide deterministic, explainable defaults for prefill and micro-batching tuned to observed traffic affinity. 2) Add a safety-first quantization fallback so tenants automatically degrade to a compatible, higher-precision variant only when quality or latency regressions are detected. 3) Surface prefilling telemetry so ops teams can validate cost/latency tradeoffs. Summary of changes:
- New runtime controller "cost_caddy" that computes an allocation hint (micro-batch shape, prefill window, cache TTL) per tenant based on short-term traffi...

title: hybrid-staged-canary-controller-with-statistical-gates-and-fallthrough-routing
preview: Motivation: enable safer multi-stage experiments and automated rollback by introducing a hybrid staged canary controller that combines time/traffic phasing, Bayesian statistical gates, and deterministic fallthrough routing. This PR implements a controller that can run A/B tests, shadow trials, and progressive canari...
text: Motivation: enable safer multi-stage experiments and automated rollback by introducing a hybrid staged canary controller that combines time/traffic phasing, Bayesian statistical gates, and deterministic fallthrough routing. This PR implements a controller that can run A/B tests, shadow trials, and progressive canaries using a configurable stability window, significance threshold, and quarantine queue. Key features: 1) BayesianSignificanceEngine (configurable priors) to decide pass/fail for metric families; 2) HealthSnapshot store that captures per-route histograms and model KV cache hit rates at sample intervals; 3) WeightDecaySchedule to gradually reduce canary traffic on persistent regressions before triggering full rollback; 4) FallthroughRouting that routes traffic to a fallback model variant instantly on hard failures while preserving request affinity; 5) QuarantineQueue and auto...

title: tokenized-fairness-scaler-and-util-gap-reporter
preview: Context and motivation: customers on Dedicated have asked for better fairness when bursts co-exist with steady throughput tenants. Existing burst pools and reservations ensure throughput but can starve latency-sensitive tenants during microbursts. This PR implements a tokenized fairness scaler: a lightweight token a...
text:
Context and motivation: customers on Dedicated have asked for better fairness when bursts co-exist with steady throughput tenants. Existing burst pools and reservations ensure throughput but can starve latency-sensitive tenants during microbursts. This PR implements a tokenized fairness scaler: a lightweight token accounting layer that mints per-tenant 'burst tokens' proportional to committed throughput and dynamically redistributes spare tokens based on queue-latency buckets.

Design summary: the scaler observes per-tenant request queue latency percentiles (p50/p95/p99) and token consumption rate. When a tenant's p95 exceeds its latency target and tokens are available in the cluster pool, the allocator grants ephemeral tokens that raise the tenant's concurrency cap for short windows (configurable TTL). Tokens are reclaimed proactively when latency recovers or when they expire. Token ...

title: Rate-limited reservation stitcher and FairBank tenancy allocator
preview: Context: Dedicated capacity is often fragmented by many small reservations and short-lived allocations. This fragmentation reduces effective utilization and drives unnecessary autoscaler churn. Previous approaches (aggressive backfilling and larger warm pools) either increased cost or exposed tenants to noisy neighb...
text:
Context: Dedicated capacity is often fragmented by many small reservations and short-lived allocations. This fragmentation reduces effective utilization and drives unnecessary autoscaler churn. Previous approaches (aggressive backfilling and larger warm pools) either increased cost or exposed tenants to noisy neighbors.

This PR implements two coordinated pieces: 1) a rate-limited 'reservation stitcher' that periodically merges adjacent, compatible reservations into larger contiguous reservation blocks to reduce fragmentation, and 2) a new tenancy allocator named 'FairBank' that uses weighted proportional allocation with soft budget banking to preserve per-tenant throughput guarantees while improving global packing efficiency.

Key design points: the stitcher only operates on reservations that are compatible by model family, region, and isolation constraints; it applies a token-bucket...

title: add difficulty-calibrator-and-heuristic-resampler-ci-hook
preview: Motivation: CI quality gates have been vulnerable to false positives/negatives when prompt sets shift subtly in difficulty (length, novelty vs. anchor set, generation entropy). This PR introduces a difficulty-calibrator component that scores prompts by a composite difficulty metric and a heuristic resampler used bot...
text: Motivation: CI quality gates have been vulnerable to false positives/negatives when prompt sets shift subtly in difficulty (length, novelty vs. anchor set, generation entropy). This PR introduces a difficulty-calibrator component that scores prompts by a composite difficulty metric and a heuristic resampler used both during local eval runs and as a CI hook to detect distributional shifts. What changed: 1) eval_harness/difficulty/calibrator.py: new module that computes a composite difficulty score per prompt (token-length-normalized surprisal proxy from a lightweight LM scoring pass, embedding distance to anchored prompt centroids, and response-model entropy estimate). 2) eval_harness/ci/hooks/difficulty_gate.py: CI hook that compares the baseline difficulty distribution (stored as a short histogram blob in CI artifacts) to the current run using a K-S style test plus a shift-aware regr...

title: Contextual weighted sample allocator and gradual restrictor for stable eval gating
preview: Motivation: CI evals have been noisy for lower-volume prompt cohorts. Teams are getting frequent false positives when a small cohort flips. This PR implements a contextual weighted sample allocator that allocates probe budget across prompt sets using a combination of: (1) prompt-level importance (manually tagged or ...
text:
Motivation: CI evals have been noisy for lower-volume prompt cohorts. Teams are getting frequent false positives when a small cohort flips. This PR implements a contextual weighted sample allocator that allocates probe budget across prompt sets using a combination of: (1) prompt-level importance (manually tagged or derived from production hit rates), (2) recent drift score (rolling 7-day), and (3) semantic novelty (via embedding distance).

Design: The allocator produces proportional sample quotas per cohort and emits a per-run sampling plan. A complementary GradualRestrictor replaces the previous immediate hard-blocking CI hook: instead of a binary fail, we compute a severity score and apply a staged response (soft-fail annotations, delayed blocking windows, and adaptive replays). This reduces churn while preserving safety for true regressions.

What changed (high level):
- New alloc...

title: add-inference-health-probe-and-sampled-kernel-queue-explorer
preview: Adds a lightweight inference health probe and a sampled kernel-queue explorer to improve incident triage and long-tail cost attribution. Motivation: recurring short-lived kernel queue stalls were noisy in SLO pages and made it hard to trace whether slow tails were caused by model-level token spikes, kernel contentio...
text: Adds a lightweight inference health probe and a sampled kernel-queue explorer to improve incident triage and long-tail cost attribution. Motivation: recurring short-lived kernel queue stalls were noisy in SLO pages and made it hard to trace whether slow tails were caused by model-level token spikes, kernel contention, or tenant-induced backpressure. This PR introduces: 1) inference_health_probe: periodic synthetic pings that exercise a small, deterministic token path and emit a health metric (inference_probe_latency_ms) plus an opaque probe_id for trace linking; 2) sampled_kernel_queue_explorer: a new trace sampler that, when a probe or real invocation exceeds a kernel-queue latency threshold, captures a compact kernel-queue snapshot and stores a compacted span with queue_depth, head_kernel_id, and estimated_token_cost_hint; 3) dashboard panels: a compact 'Probe Pulse' timeseries, ker...

title: Topology-aware peer balancing, async kernel dispatch, and KV-cache coalescing
preview: Motivation: Multi-GPU tensor-parallel workloads suffer from suboptimal collective placement and small-KV churn when requests are micro-batched across heterogeneous racks. This PR implements three coordinated runtime optimizations to improve throughput and tail latency: (1) topology-aware peer balancing: build NVLink...
text: Motivation: Multi-GPU tensor-parallel workloads suffer from suboptimal collective placement and small-KV churn when requests are micro-batched across heterogeneous racks. This PR implements three coordinated runtime optimizations to improve throughput and tail latency: (1) topology-aware peer balancing: build NVLink/NIC-aware ring/graph membership using a lightweight port-speed probe so collectives prefer intra-node/NVLink lanes and minimize cross-node hops, and dynamically rebalance peer pairs when per-step bandwidth estimates indicate hotlinks; (2) async kernel dispatch: move per-layer kernel launch to a dispatch thread pair that annotates CUDA streams with priority and orders kernels for overlap with NCCL comms, and add a non-blocking enqueue-locality check so compute work can start as soon as required dependencies are queued; (3) KV-cache coalescing at the request router: group co...

title: stabilize-stream-framing-and-introduce-retry-hint-headers-for-tool-calls
preview: Motivation: Production customers observed intermittent stream desynchronization for tool/function-call flows and inconsistent client retry behavior across OpenAI-compatible adapters. This PR stabilizes server->client stream framing for tool calls by adding explicit lifecycle frames and improves retry semantics by in...
text: Motivation: Production customers observed intermittent stream desynchronization for tool/function-call flows and inconsistent client retry behavior across OpenAI-compatible adapters. This PR stabilizes server->client stream framing for tool calls by adding explicit lifecycle frames and improves retry semantics by introducing a machine-friendly Retry-Hint header plus an Idempotency-Timestamp header to disambiguate partial successes. The change set: 1) Stream framing: add two lightweight frame types ("heartbeat" and "finalize") to the streaming protocol used for function/tool outputs so clients have deterministic markers for request liveness and end-of-output; this fixes race conditions where clients treated reconnects as duplicates. 2) Retry hints: add Retry-Hint header (JSON-safe string with recommended backoff window and idempotency-sensitivity tag) emitted on 429/503/504 errors and ...

title: hybrid flashattn + lazy-kv-eviction + operator-fusion for long-seq workloads
preview: Motivation: Production workloads with mixed short- and long-context requests show uneven tail latency and high aggregate token cost due to conservative KV cache retention and generic attention kernel selection. This PR introduces a hybrid attention path that (1) dispatches FlashAttention when safe, (2) uses a lightw...
text:
Motivation: Production workloads with mixed short- and long-context requests show uneven tail latency and high aggregate token cost due to conservative KV cache retention and generic attention kernel selection. This PR introduces a hybrid attention path that (1) dispatches FlashAttention when safe, (2) uses a lightweight fused matmul-add-dropout kernel for mid-sized heads, and (3) falls back to the existing fallback kernel for pathological shapes. In parallel, we implement a lazy KV eviction policy that decouples KV entry lifetime from request completion and enables opportunistic eviction under memory pressure. Together these changes reduce long-sequence p99 latency and improve throughput for streaming workloads while keeping memory bounded.

What changed (high level): implemented a Flash-aware scheduler that picks kernel variant at invocation time based on sequence length, head size,...

title: Introduce hierarchical fidelity oracle and non-blocking CI adapter
preview: Motivation: existing CI gates are brittle for prompt sets with heterogeneous lengths, multi-turn contexts and low-frequency anchors which lead to noisy false positives. This PR introduces a hierarchical fidelity oracle that fuses multiple evaluation signals (semantic similarity, role-aware turn consistency, token-le...
text: Motivation: existing CI gates are brittle for prompt sets with heterogeneous lengths, multi-turn contexts and low-frequency anchors which lead to noisy false positives. This PR introduces a hierarchical fidelity oracle that fuses multiple evaluation signals (semantic similarity, role-aware turn consistency, token-level saliency, and anchor stability) across temporal cohorts and prompt granularity, and exposes a non-blocking CI adapter that surfaces soft-fail quality regressions with prioritized remediation suggestions. Core behaviors: 1) compute per-prompt multi-signal fidelity vectors, 2) aggregate per-cohort with recency-aware weighting and uncertainty calibration, 3) emit composite fidelity score and explainability hints (top contributing tokens and failing turns), 4) provide a non-blocking CI decision path which fails only on high-confidence regressions and otherwise annotates PRs...

title: Mini-bisect canary with traffic snapshots and automatic warmstart rollback oracle
preview: Summary: Introduce a lightweight 'mini-bisect' canary mode and traffic snapshotting to speed up diagnosis and automate rollback for warmstart regressions. Motivation: We observed slow, manual bisect workflows when a canary exhibits intermittent regressions related to cache warmstart/kv-cache priming. This PR impleme...
text: Summary: Introduce a lightweight 'mini-bisect' canary mode and traffic snapshotting to speed up diagnosis and automate rollback for warmstart regressions. Motivation: We observed slow, manual bisect workflows when a canary exhibits intermittent regressions related to cache warmstart/kv-cache priming. This PR implements a controller pattern that runs short-window bisect-style probes, records traffic snapshots (input distribution, sequence-length buckets, metadata tags), and evaluates a compact set of metric comparisons to decide an automatic rollback. Design: 1) controller: new MiniBisectController that coordinates N short probe waves (default 3), each wave splits a small, orthogonal slice of traffic to the candidate model. 2) traffic snapshots: proto-compact JSON blobs capturing token-length histograms, request metadata tags, and sampled request hashes; snapshots are stored in the rol...

title: typed errors + ergonomic iterable reconnect API across SDKs
preview: Motivation: Developers repeatedly hit vague runtime errors when streams failed (auth handshake, transient network) and had to implement ad-hoc parsing logic to decide whether to retry, resume, or surface an error. This PR introduces language-native typed error classes across the three SDKs (Python/TypeScript/Go) and...
text:
Motivation: Developers repeatedly hit vague runtime errors when streams failed (auth handshake, transient network) and had to implement ad-hoc parsing logic to decide whether to retry, resume, or surface an error. This PR introduces language-native typed error classes across the three SDKs (Python/TypeScript/Go) and a unified ergonomic iterable reconnect API for stream consumers.

What this change does: 
- Introduces TypedError/StreamError subclasses with machine-friendly error_code, retriable, and suggestion fields across SDKs.
- Adds a new StreamIterable API (python: StreamIterable, ts: createStreamIterable, go: StreamIterator) that yields typed result objects and automatically attempts a single reconnect on retriable failures by default with backoff tuning. Reconnect behavior is configurable by the caller.
- Deprecates low-level sync callback hooks (on_chunk/on_error) in favor of a...

title: Tenant ecoregion slowpath isolation and soft-park emergency stabilization
preview: Summary: Introduces a serving-side scheduler extension that groups tenants into lightweight 'ecoregions' (region + workload fingerprint) and isolates slowpath/large-KV sessions into a soft-park queue when node-level KV pressure threatens SLOs. Motivation: During the Jan incident we observed correlated KV-heavy warm ...
text: Summary: Introduces a serving-side scheduler extension that groups tenants into lightweight 'ecoregions' (region + workload fingerprint) and isolates slowpath/large-KV sessions into a soft-park queue when node-level KV pressure threatens SLOs. Motivation: During the Jan incident we observed correlated KV-heavy warm sessions from multiple tenants in the same AZ causing tail latency spikes and subsequent SLO breaches for unrelated low-latency tenants. This change provides a fast mitigation: (1) detect sustained KV pressure per-node and per-ecoregion, (2) move new/remaining slowpath requests for affected ecoregions into a soft-park state (deferred processing + light-weight token), (3) prioritize unmoved requests to preserve 95th/99th latency for interactive tenants, and (4) expose observability + an operator rollback. This PR is scoped for runtime safety and is intended as an emergency s...

title: introduce-slateline-batch-lanes-and-hierarchical-quant-allocator
preview: Motivation: Large fleets see varying request shapes; a single batching policy + flat quant profile leaves money on the table. This PR introduces 'SlateLine' batch lanes and a hierarchical quant allocator that together provide per-tenant/per-workload batch shaping and a priority-ranked quant fallback tree to reduce u...
text: Motivation: Large fleets see varying request shapes; a single batching policy + flat quant profile leaves money on the table. This PR introduces 'SlateLine' batch lanes and a hierarchical quant allocator that together provide per-tenant/per-workload batch shaping and a priority-ranked quant fallback tree to reduce unit cost while respecting SLOs. Design summary: SlateLine divides the serving runtime batcher into lightweight lanes (fast-latency lane, balanced lane, throughput lane) with a tiny per-lane selector that uses recent shape histograms and token budget hints to pick the lane for each request. The hierarchical quant allocator maps model variants into a tree of quantization profiles: top nodes are high-fidelity (FP16/quant8-mixed) and leaves are aggressive int4/int2 variants. At runtime the allocator prefers higher-fidelity nodes but can step down along the tree when budget or c...

title: Introduce jurisdictional operator claims, KMS proxy with region affinity, and audit canonicalizer
preview: Motivation: operator-initiated requests that used fallback KMS routes could violate tenant residency expectations in multi-geozone deployments. This PR introduces a lightweight KMS proxy with region-affinity routing and a jurisdictional operator-claim propagation middleware that ties RBAC v2 decisions to geozone cla...
text: Motivation: operator-initiated requests that used fallback KMS routes could violate tenant residency expectations in multi-geozone deployments. This PR introduces a lightweight KMS proxy with region-affinity routing and a jurisdictional operator-claim propagation middleware that ties RBAC v2 decisions to geozone claims. It also adds an audit header canonicalizer to ensure consistent downstream proofs for compliance ingestion. Changes include a claim backfill migration for existing operator sessions and telemetry for residency decision sampling. Checklist: - [x] design doc and Linear ticket ENG-4521 linked, - [x] RBAC v2 policy and policy tests, - [x] KMS proxy and unit tests, - [x] migration/backfill job and smoke tests, - [x] staged rollout flag + installer hook, - [ ] manual staging canary and runbook signoff. Design references: https://linear.app/redwood/issue/ENG-4521 and internal...

title: unify-toolchain-bootstraps-and-introduce-lightweight-presubmit
preview: Motivation: Our repo currently has three slightly different developer bootstraps (lint, unit-test, integration-smoke) implemented as independent scripts and Dockerfiles which diverged over time. This PR consolidates those bootstraps into a single lightweight, hermetic toolchain image and a small Go-based presubmit r...
text: Motivation: Our repo currently has three slightly different developer bootstraps (lint, unit-test, integration-smoke) implemented as independent scripts and Dockerfiles which diverged over time. This PR consolidates those bootstraps into a single lightweight, hermetic toolchain image and a small Go-based presubmit runner which selects the minimal runtime surface at CI time. Goals: reduce duplication, speed cold CI starts, make dependency updates atomic, and simplify contributor onboarding. What this change does: 1) Introduces tools/toolchain/manifest.yaml describing canonical toolchain components and pins (node, go, python, buildx). 2) Adds images/toolchain/Dockerfile which builds a single slim toolchain image used by lint and test runners. 3) Replaces three bash bootstraps with cmd/presubmit-runner (Go) which performs staged execution: install-verify -> lint -> unit-tests -> integrat...

title: Introduce epochal log tiling and cost-gradient alert smoother
preview: Motivation: Recent incidents showed noisy long-tail log retention and spiky cost alerts that produced pager fatigue. This PR implements two complementary subsystems to reduce noise while preserving fidelity for root-cause: 1) epochal log tiling: a retention/compaction scheme that buckets invocation logs into time-an...
text:
Motivation: Recent incidents showed noisy long-tail log retention and spiky cost alerts that produced pager fatigue. This PR implements two complementary subsystems to reduce noise while preserving fidelity for root-cause: 1) epochal log tiling: a retention/compaction scheme that buckets invocation logs into time-anchored 'epochs' and keeps variable-fidelity tiles (high-fidelity for recent epochs, compacted digests for older epochs). 2) cost-gradient alert smoother: an adaptive alert thresholding algorithm that considers the short-term gradient of per-tenant token cost to suppress transient spikes and surface persistent regressions.

What this PR changes (summary): - New epochal tiler microservice (observability/log-tiler) with configurable epoch length and tile retention policy. - Retention controller that coordinates between object store (S3/GCS) and hot cache to keep recent tiles a...

title: Fold per-layer scale into inline requantizer; attention KV-cache compatibility
preview: Motivation: INT8 and FP8 paths pay a per-token cost for explicit scale/dequant round-trips in the attention kernel. This PR folds stable per-layer/chunk scaling factors into an inline requantizer inside the GEMM/attention kernels and adds compatibility glue for our attention KV-cache (so cached keys/values don't tri...
text: Motivation: INT8 and FP8 paths pay a per-token cost for explicit scale/dequant round-trips in the attention kernel. This PR folds stable per-layer/chunk scaling factors into an inline requantizer inside the GEMM/attention kernels and adds compatibility glue for our attention KV-cache (so cached keys/values don't trigger extra conversions on read). Goals: reduce per-token arithmetic, improve single-stream latency, and keep cached tensors bit-compatible across quant/float fallbacks. Implementation summary: 1) introduce a lightweight FoldedScale descriptor that is emitted at model-load time and stored alongside quant metadata; 2) implement inline_requantizer.{cc,hh} which applies scale folding + rounding once on kernel entry and keeps activations in the requantized layout for the kernel duration; 3) modify attention KV load/store paths to check for FoldedScale and avoid redundant dequant...

title: phase-shifted-latency-sweep-and-coldstart-prober
preview: Motivation: We need a lightweight, reproducible runner that exercises both steady-state and phase-shifted burst patterns while simultaneously measuring cold-start latency across different model variants and runtime kernel configs. This PR introduces a phase-shifted latency sweep runner and a paired cold-start prober...
text:
Motivation: We need a lightweight, reproducible runner that exercises both steady-state and phase-shifted burst patterns while simultaneously measuring cold-start latency across different model variants and runtime kernel configs. This PR introduces a phase-shifted latency sweep runner and a paired cold-start prober that can be composed to produce joint distributions (latency vs cold-start tail) used by the regression detector pipeline.
Design:
- phase_sweep_runner: Python runner that generates a sequence of workload phases with configurable duration, RPS, sequence length distribution and an adjustable phase-offset parameter to produce controlled interleaving across client shards.
- coldstart_prober: small Go probe that issues warmup-less single-shot requests on a configurable model selection (quantized/FP16/FP32) to capture cold-start percentiles under concurrent load.
- reporter bri...

title: Add Cost Model API Grafana dashboards (latency/errors, schema version dist, drift)
preview: Summary: Adds a Grafana folder + dashboards for the Cost Model API so Console/Optimize rollouts have a single place to monitor health, adoption, and correctness drift. Includes panels for request volume, p50/p95/p99 latency, error-rate by code, schema version distribution, request-bounds rejections, cache hit/miss (...
text: Summary: Adds a Grafana folder + dashboards for the Cost Model API so Console/Optimize rollouts have a single place to monitor health, adoption, and correctness drift. Includes panels for request volume, p50/p95/p99 latency, error-rate by code, schema version distribution, request-bounds rejections, cache hit/miss (client-side + service memoization), and a first pass of drift metrics (predicted vs observed) sourced from the scheduled drift job. Motivation: During early Console integration testing we saw two recurring issues: (1) latency spikes due to cardinality blowups on hot paths and (2) drift alerts firing due to telemetry lag vs genuine mismatch. This PR standardizes the dashboards and bakes in guardrail signals (telemetry freshness, missing inputs) so oncall can quickly triage. Related: redwood/pr-28457 (service endpoints), redwood/pr-28490 (drift monitoring job), redwood/pr-284...

title: Instrument kernel-level latency fingerprints and introduce adaptive kernel-fingerprint alerting
preview: Motivation: We see occasional long-tail invocation latency where root cause is GPU kernel scheduling/serialization rather than model token path. Existing observability surfaces capture per-invocation token timing but lack a compact way to correlate GPU kernel-level skew across tenants and hardware. This PR instrumen...
text:
Motivation: We see occasional long-tail invocation latency where root cause is GPU kernel scheduling/serialization rather than model token path. Existing observability surfaces capture per-invocation token timing but lack a compact way to correlate GPU kernel-level skew across tenants and hardware. This PR instruments low-level kernel timing as compact 'fingerprints', surfaces a kernel heatmap dashboard, and introduces an adaptive alert that suppresses noise during autoscale events.

What this PR does (high-level):
- Adds runtime span attributes: kernel_name, kernel_ms, gpu_sm_id, kernel_queue_depth. Spans are emitted by the serving runtime when kernels are dispatched.
- Emits a new metric series: telemetry.kernel_fingerprint with labels {model_family, hw_class, kernel_name, tenant_id? (opt)}, using reservoir sampling to limit cardinality.
- Adds a Kernel Heatmap dashboard (dashboards...

title: consolidate-premerge-linters-and-ci-shepherd-for-dep-trimming
preview: Motivation: our presubmit plane has accumulated 6 separate linter/test entrypoints that slow fast feedback and confuse contributors. This PR centralizes linting into a single lightweight 'ci-shepherd' composite action, migrates legacy shell-run linters to a typed Node wrapper, and introduces a dependent-presubmit tr...
text: Motivation: our presubmit plane has accumulated 6 separate linter/test entrypoints that slow fast feedback and confuse contributors. This PR centralizes linting into a single lightweight 'ci-shepherd' composite action, migrates legacy shell-run linters to a typed Node wrapper, and introduces a dependent-presubmit trim step that excludes irrelevant packages from heavyweight integration tests. Changeset summary: (1) new actions/ci-shepherd composite with stage-aware lanes (fast-lint, slow-lint, unit-smoke), (2) migrate eslint/remark invocations into the node-based runner to allow deterministic caching and telemetry, (3) add .ci/dep-trim/indexer that computes minimal test matrix from changed files (opt-in via PR label), (4) update docs/CONTRIBUTING.md and devcontainer instructions, (5) pin a handful of devdeps and update lockfiles to reduce transient failures. Why this matters: median pr...

title: v1/chat: output coordinator + role-pruner for compact structured tool responses
preview: Motivation: customers relying on OpenAI /v1/chat.completions compatibility have increasingly complex tool/function outputs and varied role labels from legacy clients. The streaming path was producing verbose, repeated JSON fragments for tool responses and leaking low-confidence token-level logprobs during long strea...
text:
Motivation: customers relying on OpenAI /v1/chat.completions compatibility have increasingly complex tool/function outputs and varied role labels from legacy clients. The streaming path was producing verbose, repeated JSON fragments for tool responses and leaking low-confidence token-level logprobs during long streams. This PR introduces an 'output coordinator' layer in the v1 chat adapter and a conservative 'role-pruner' to normalize incoming roles and emit a compact structured mode for tool/function outputs.

Summary of changes: added an adapter-level coordinator that: 1) recognizes and collapses repeated tool JSON fragments into a single compact structure when clients request compact output (new query param compact_output=true), 2) supplies a configurable stop_hint_priority so that explicit stop sequences sent by the API call are respected ahead of model-internal heuristics when de...

title: generate delta upgrade index and SBOM stamping for private-installer CI
preview: This PR adds a CI-first pipeline to produce deterministic delta upgrade packages and stamp SBOM/provenance metadata into the private-installer release artifacts. Motivation: customers running Private (VPC/on-prem) need compact upgrade bundles and a verifiable upgrade graph so operators can perform safe roll-forwards...
text: This PR adds a CI-first pipeline to produce deterministic delta upgrade packages and stamp SBOM/provenance metadata into the private-installer release artifacts. Motivation: customers running Private (VPC/on-prem) need compact upgrade bundles and a verifiable upgrade graph so operators can perform safe roll-forwards and rollbacks without re-pulling full images. The output of the pipeline is a signed delta-index (delta-index.json) that expresses available upgrade paths between installer versions and references per-artifact SBOMs, Helm chart lockfiles, and Terraform module plan snapshots. Changes include: 1) new pipeline job (private-installer:delta-pack) that builds compact tarballs containing only changed manifests and image digests; 2) delta-indexer tool (tools/release/delta-indexer.go) which computes graph edges and emits SBOM references; 3) SBOM stamping step using syft and a light...

title: Granular SLO fallback credit allocator and soft cutover for regional failovers
preview: Context: Recent incidents showed that coarse global failover decisions cause blunt throttles where small tenants receive disproportionate evictions, and noisy tenants exhaust regional failover budgets leading to cascading circuit trips. This PR introduces a per-tenant 'fallback credit' allocator and a soft cutover p...
text: Context: Recent incidents showed that coarse global failover decisions cause blunt throttles where small tenants receive disproportionate evictions, and noisy tenants exhaust regional failover budgets leading to cascading circuit trips. This PR introduces a per-tenant 'fallback credit' allocator and a soft cutover pathway for failover windows so regional handoffs can be gradual and observable. Motivation: provide finer-grained SLO enforcement during overloads, reduce noisy-tenant blast radius, and allow gradual regional traffic rebalancing without immediate hard deny or full circuit flips. Summary of changes: 1) implement fallback_credit_store (in-memory sharded) that tracks per-tenant credits consumed during soft-fallback windows; credits replenish on a tunable epoch and are weighted by tenant SLO tier and recent success rate; 2) add a credit-aware admission filter in the regional ro...

title: fp8-autotune-dequant-rewrite-and-kernel-router
preview: Motivation: We observed cache-sensitive and sequence-length-dependent regressions when serving FP8/INT8 model variants on the high-throughput path. Two root causes surfaced in profiling: (1) redundant dequantization stubs inserted by the compiler leading to extra memory traffic across the KV cache boundary, and (2) ...
text:
Motivation: We observed cache-sensitive and sequence-length-dependent regressions when serving FP8/INT8 model variants on the high-throughput path. Two root causes surfaced in profiling: (1) redundant dequantization stubs inserted by the compiler leading to extra memory traffic across the KV cache boundary, and (2) suboptimal kernel selection for mixed FP8/INT8 attention matmuls at mid-sequence lengths, which increases both latency tail and per-token GPU utilization. This PR introduces an autotuned FP8 dequant rewrite pass (dequant-rewriter) plus a scheduler-aware kernel router that picks kernels based on runtime signals (sequence position, KV cache hit pattern, and batching profile).

Summary of changes:
- Implement dequant-rewriter: a graph-level pass that coalesces adjacent dequant ops, hoists dequantization out of hot KV cache paths when safe, and emits a fused "dequant+transpose"...

title: Dependency-aware rollout orchestrator with safety chains and cohort quarantine
preview: Motivation: We frequently run multi-model experiments (A/B tests, shadow+canary hybrids) where some models are dependent on upstream feature transformations or other model variants. Current rollouts treat each variant independently which can lead to invalid traffic splits and unsafe rollbacks when dependent cohorts ...
text:
Motivation: We frequently run multi-model experiments (A/B tests, shadow+canary hybrids) where some models are dependent on upstream feature transformations or other model variants. Current rollouts treat each variant independently which can lead to invalid traffic splits and unsafe rollbacks when dependent cohorts are promoted or quarantined. This PR introduces a dependency-aware rollout orchestrator that models 'safety chains' between rollout nodes so that promotion/rollback decisions are propagated deterministically across related cohorts.

Summary of changes: Adds a new orchestration component (dependency orchestrator) to the servegraph pipeline, implements safety-chain semantics, and extends the rollout policy language with two new directives: "requires" (declare dependencies) and "quarantine_on_failure" (automatic cohort isolation). The orchestrator supports: 1) transactional co...

title: openai-v1-chat-params-clarity-and-toolcall-debounce-for-stream-stability
preview: Motivation: We continue to close the gap between Redwood's /v1/chat compatibility surface and the OpenAI chat/completions expectations. Customers reported two classes of issues: (1) noisy rapid fncall footprints during network retries and websocket reconnects that caused multiple tool invocations, and (2) ambiguous ...
text:
Motivation: We continue to close the gap between Redwood's /v1/chat compatibility surface and the OpenAI chat/completions expectations. Customers reported two classes of issues: (1) noisy rapid fncall footprints during network retries and websocket reconnects that caused multiple tool invocations, and (2) ambiguous behavior when multiple stop signals, role aliases, or logprob fallbacks co-occurred in streaming output. This PR implements two coordinated changes: (A) a parameter clarity layer that normalizes and documents v1 chat params (role aliases, stop token semantics, logprob backoff) so downstream stages get a deterministic shape; (B) a lightweight toolcall debounce and stream heartbeat handshake that prevents duplicated function/tool invocations on transient reconnects and provides a clearer stream parity signal to clients. 

What changed (high level):
- Added a v1 param canonica...

title: Add bridged on-call context and automated escalation templates
preview: Motivation: on-call responders frequently need full incident context when incidents are bridged across multiple chat channels and paging systems; manual copy/paste is error-prone and slows TTR. This PR introduces a compact, serializable "bridged context" snapshot that travels with the incident across channels and an...
text: Motivation: on-call responders frequently need full incident context when incidents are bridged across multiple chat channels and paging systems; manual copy/paste is error-prone and slows TTR. This PR introduces a compact, serializable "bridged context" snapshot that travels with the incident across channels and an opinionated library of parameterized escalation templates that the bot can surface or send automatically. Changes include: 1) context capture: snapshot of current runbook pointer, top-3 alerts, recent error rates, and active mitigations; 2) context propagation: add signed metadata token to bridged messages so downstream responders receive identical context; 3) template library: YAML-driven templates with conditional rendering, throttling and dedupe keys; 4) escalation rules: configurable selectors that map alert signals + SLO breach state to template choices and escalation...

title: decompose-test-profiles-and-add-dependency-triage-bot
preview:
Title: Decompose test profiles and add dependency triage bot

Description: This PR refactors CI test sharding into small, profile-aware shards and introduces an automated dependency triage bot that opens recommended bump PRs and labels candidate repos for manual review. Motivation: long CI queues and noisy full-suit...
text:
Title: Decompose test profiles and add dependency triage bot

Description: This PR refactors CI test sharding into small, profile-aware shards and introduces an automated dependency triage bot that opens recommended bump PRs and labels candidate repos for manual review. Motivation: long CI queues and noisy full-suite runs made iteration expensive. By splitting test shards by runtime characteristics (fast/unit, medium/integration, slow/e2e) and making the dependency update workflow proactive we reduce queue contention and accelerate dependency hygiene.

What this changes (high level):
- CI: introduce three explicit shard profiles (fast/unit, medium/integration, slow/e2e) and a lightweight profile selector that picks a shard subset based on changed paths and heuristics.
- CI: add path-aware test runner so lint and unit tests only run for affected packages when the change is scoped.
- CI...

title: Invoke compatibility proto mapper and decision telemetry
preview: Motivation: Customers expect OpenAI-style function/tool calling to 'just work' when routed through Redwood's serving runtime. However, in practice we see a set of recurring issues: heterogeneous tool descriptors from third-party models, ambiguous tool_choice semantics when multiple tools match, and stream fragmentat...
text: Motivation: Customers expect OpenAI-style function/tool calling to 'just work' when routed through Redwood's serving runtime. However, in practice we see a set of recurring issues: heterogeneous tool descriptors from third-party models, ambiguous tool_choice semantics when multiple tools match, and stream fragmentation that splits the tool invocation payload across SSE boundaries. This PR introduces a compatibility-focused proto mapper and decision telemetry that together make tool invocation more deterministic and observable.Design summary: 1) proto_mapper: a lightweight rule engine that canonicalizes incoming OpenAI-compatible invocation envelopes into a single Redwood internal representation. It normalizes fields (name/description/schema/arguments), expands legacy aliases into canonical redwood_tool_id, and attaches a compat_hint when the mapping required heuristics. Mappings are d...

title: Introduce latency-distribution-collector and histogram-exporter for perf runs
preview: Motivation: our current perf suite reports mean/median p50/p95/p99 but lacks a first-class histogram distribution export that lets ops and product slices examine tail behavior by bucket and request class. This PR introduces a latency-distribution-collector (benchmarks/runner/latency_collector.py) and a histogram_exp...
text: Motivation: our current perf suite reports mean/median p50/p95/p99 but lacks a first-class histogram distribution export that lets ops and product slices examine tail behavior by bucket and request class. This PR introduces a latency-distribution-collector (benchmarks/runner/latency_collector.py) and a histogram_exporter that emits fixed-window histograms and a Prometheus-compatible scrape endpoint for aggregated CI runs. Key changes: 1) adds a thread-safe latency_collector that supports labeled buckets (model, route, sequence_length_bin) and controlled flush intervals; 2) implements histogram_exporter with configurable bucket boundaries and JSON/Prometheus output modes; 3) integrates collection step into ci/perf-pipeline.yml so nightly jobs persist artifacts to the perf artifacts store; 4) adds validation tests and an examples script to reproduce locally. Implementation notes: the co...

title: evidence-pack-assembler-and-runbook-heuristics
preview: Summary: This change adds an "evidence pack" assembler to the incident-bot pipeline and a set of runbook heuristics to automatically curate the most relevant diagnostics for an alert. The assembler collects contextual artifacts (metrics snapshots, recent logs, trace spans, KV cache summaries, and SLO delta snapshots...
text:
Summary: This change adds an "evidence pack" assembler to the incident-bot pipeline and a set of runbook heuristics to automatically curate the most relevant diagnostics for an alert. The assembler collects contextual artifacts (metrics snapshots, recent logs, trace spans, KV cache summaries, and SLO delta snapshots), ranks them using lightweight heuristics, prunes/pivots sensitive fields, and generates a compressed evidence bundle that can be attached to runbooks and pager notifications.

Motivation and context: On long-running incidents engineers spend a lot of time pulling the same set of data from multiple systems for triage. This PR is targeted at reducing mean time to acknowledgement and the handoff friction by producing a focused, size-bounded artifact that the oncall and responder can consume in the first 10 minutes. It also standardizes what we include for downstream postmort...

title: add-prefetch-latency-matrix-and-alert-accordion
preview: Context: We've observed recurring small latency spikes during prefetch/warmup warm paths that do not trigger existing SLO alerts because they are below coarse aggregation thresholds. Motivation: Provide finer-grained visibility into prefetch-phase latency and introduce an alert 'accordion' that gracefully widens or ...
text: Context: We've observed recurring small latency spikes during prefetch/warmup warm paths that do not trigger existing SLO alerts because they are below coarse aggregation thresholds. Motivation: Provide finer-grained visibility into prefetch-phase latency and introduce an alert 'accordion' that gracefully widens or narrows a route's alert sensitivity based on correlated signals (kv-cache hit rate, kernel-queue depth, and tenant priority). What this PR does: 1) Adds a Prefetch Latency Matrix metric pipeline (histogram shards per 50ms slab) to capture warmup latency per invocation phase and per-tenant micro-slice; 2) Adds a dashboard JSON for the matrix with cohorting controls (route, model, quantization profile, tenant tier) and interactive sparkline overlays for kernel queue and KV occupancy; 3) Implements an Alert Accordion policy in alerts/accordion_policy.go that composes multi-sig...

title: Circuit stabilizer: connection quieting and tenant SLO-tilt hotpatch
preview: Motivation: During an incident on 2026-02-14 we observed rapid circuit reopen/reclose oscillations for certain islanded regions which amplified load and caused tenant-level SLO violations. Root cause analysis showed two compounding issues: (1) the circuit reconciler could race when reconciling restored health probes...
text: Motivation: During an incident on 2026-02-14 we observed rapid circuit reopen/reclose oscillations for certain islanded regions which amplified load and caused tenant-level SLO violations. Root cause analysis showed two compounding issues: (1) the circuit reconciler could race when reconciling restored health probes and reopen attempts, and (2) the shedder decision logic treated all SLO deficits equally, causing aggressive shedding for tenants where small latency increases were acceptable and for others where impact was severe. This PR introduces a targeted hotpatch that (a) adds a short 'connection quieting' window after a circuit state change to prevent immediate reopen retries, (b) introduces a tenant SLO-tilt score that biases the regional shedder away from high-impact tenants when feasible, and (c) fixes the reconcilation race by serializing state transitions through a single rec...

title: adaptive-capacity-harmonizer-and-fallback-inspector-hotfix
preview: Motivation: We observed a multi-region incident where aggressive rejoin behavior and aggressive failback caused repeated oscillations of traffic between regions. This triggered SLO thrashing and higher tail latency for customers routed through small-capacity regions. This hotfix introduces two coordinated mechanisms...
text: Motivation: We observed a multi-region incident where aggressive rejoin behavior and aggressive failback caused repeated oscillations of traffic between regions. This triggered SLO thrashing and higher tail latency for customers routed through small-capacity regions. This hotfix introduces two coordinated mechanisms: an adaptive capacity harmonizer (server-side quota reservoir + per-region rejoin damping) and a sticky fallback inspector (diagnostics + short-term sticky fallback policy) to prevent rapid oscillation during regional recovery windows. Summary of changes: implement quota reservoir to reserve a tiny emergency budget when a region reports healthy but not yet warmed KV caches; add rejoin damping (cooldown fence + exponential rejoin backoff) to the node lifecycle; expose fallback-inspector endpoint for on-call debugging and live decisions; wire new metrics (fallback.harmonizer...

title: introduce kernel-variance prober to map per-layer sequence-length inefficiencies
preview: Motivation: We frequently see tail latency regressions that only appear for particular sequence-length distributions. Existing workload runners produce aggregate token-level metrics but do not attribute variance to model kernels or layers. This PR adds a 'kernel-variance prober' that runs focused microbenchmarks acr...
text: Motivation: We frequently see tail latency regressions that only appear for particular sequence-length distributions. Existing workload runners produce aggregate token-level metrics but do not attribute variance to model kernels or layers. This PR adds a 'kernel-variance prober' that runs focused microbenchmarks across sequence length windows and collects fine-grained per-kernel timing and variance profiles so we can prioritize kernel/graph-level optimizations and targeted quantization tradeoffs. Change summary: - New binary tools/bench/prober/kernel_variance_prober.py: orchestrates probing campaigns across sequence-length buckets, random seeds, and batch shapes; supports warmup/steady-state detection and adaptive sampling. - Benchmark harness extensions bench/harness/sequence_sampler.py: sequence-length aware samplers and an API to plug sampling policies into existing runners. - Prof...

title: introduce-lightweight-local-dev-runner-and-toolchain-stub
preview: Context and motivation: onboarding and fast feedback for local CI iterations has regressed as our CI matrix grew. This PR introduces a lightweight local dev runner (tools/fastdev) and a minimal toolchain stub (dev/Toolchain.toml) plus a small workflow that developers can run locally or in ephemeral self-hosted runne...
text: Context and motivation: onboarding and fast feedback for local CI iterations has regressed as our CI matrix grew. This PR introduces a lightweight local dev runner (tools/fastdev) and a minimal toolchain stub (dev/Toolchain.toml) plus a small workflow that developers can run locally or in ephemeral self-hosted runners to get the same sanity checks as CI without the full matrix. This is an opt-in developer convenience feature that reduces time-to-first-green and iteration time on lint/test changes. Summary of changes: - add tools/fastdev/runner.go: small Go binary that runs a subset of checks in-process with conservative timeouts and filesystem caching - add tools/fastdev/README.md: usage, examples, and how to wire into VS Code tasks - add dev/Toolchain.toml: lightweight list of pinned tool versions for local dev (go, node, python, prettier, husky minimal config) - add .github/workflow...

title: Attenuated warm-pool smoothing and utilization exposer for dedicated fleets
preview: Context: Dedicated fleet warm pools are currently susceptible to short-lived microbursts: a handful of tenants generate transient spikes that cause the warm-pool controller to over-allocate headroom, inflate cost, and produce noisy per-tenant utilization metrics. This PR introduces an attenuated smoothing policy for...
text: Context: Dedicated fleet warm pools are currently susceptible to short-lived microbursts: a handful of tenants generate transient spikes that cause the warm-pool controller to over-allocate headroom, inflate cost, and produce noisy per-tenant utilization metrics. This PR introduces an attenuated smoothing policy for warm-pool sizing and a lightweight utilization exposer that decouples instantaneous spikes from the long-running reservation logic. Motivation: reduce oscillation-driven overprovisioning, improve stability for tenant SLAs, and provide clearer telemetry to platform teams so they can tune reservations and burst contracts. Changes in this PR (high level): - Introduce an attenuated smoothing window with configurable decay factors and min/max headroom bounds. The algorithm applies exponential decay to short spikes while allowing sustained demand to grow warm pool capacity. - Ad...

title: Cost Model API: add policy guardrails for what-if projections (bounds + allowed ranges)
preview: Context: The new Cost Model what-if endpoint is intentionally flexible (batching/caching/quantization knobs) and will be called by Optimize (interactive) and potentially Console (hot paths). In early staging tests and after SUP-31877 triage, we saw two failure modes: (1) callers passing extreme parameter values (e.g...
text: Context: The new Cost Model what-if endpoint is intentionally flexible (batching/caching/quantization knobs) and will be called by Optimize (interactive) and potentially Console (hot paths). In early staging tests and after SUP-31877 triage, we saw two failure modes: (1) callers passing extreme parameter values (e.g., batch size 4096, cache warm-rate > 1.0) leading to unrealistic savings outputs, and (2) expensive computations when callers submit high-cardinality workload summaries (large histograms / too many route keys) that can blow our latency budget and increase tail p95s. This PR adds explicit policy guardrails at the API boundary so the service is safe-by-default, deterministic, and bounded. Summary of changes: (A) Introduce a centralized PolicyGuardrails layer for what-if requests that validates and clamps input ranges (where clamping is explicitly allowed) and rejects invalid...

title: Introduce telemetry-driven synthetic load generator and hyperops for batch profile autotuning
preview: Problem: Production telemetry indicates wide variance in per-customer request shape (seq lengths, prompt/templates, temperature) which makes static batching defaults suboptimal. We need a way to synthesize representative load from existing traces, run lightweight microbenchmark passes, and generate conservative batc...
text: Problem: Production telemetry indicates wide variance in per-customer request shape (seq lengths, prompt/templates, temperature) which makes static batching defaults suboptimal. We need a way to synthesize representative load from existing traces, run lightweight microbenchmark passes, and generate conservative batch/cache/quantization profiles that are safe for rollout. This PR implements a telemetry-driven 'synthetic-econ-proxy' tool plus runtime hyperops that drive an autotuner. Goals: 1) generate reproducible synthetic streams from sampled traces, 2) run a fast hyperparameter sweep for batch sizes, microbatch windows, and cache TTLs in a sandbox mode, 3) attach a compact 'handoff' metadata blob to candidate profiles for safe staged rollout and automatic rollback. Key features: - synthetic-econ-proxy CLI: consumes sampled telemetry (p50/p95 seq distribution, client headers, model f...

title: per-request graceful-degradation: kernel compatibility shim + precision-hint API
preview: Motivation: We continue to see tail latency regressions in mixed-precision kernels when models with large kv-cache footprints are routed through the 'fast' execution path. This PR introduces a lightweight compatibility shim and a per-request 'precision hint' header that lets the router/scheduler choose a conservativ...
text:
Motivation: We continue to see tail latency regressions in mixed-precision kernels when models with large kv-cache footprints are routed through the 'fast' execution path. This PR introduces a lightweight compatibility shim and a per-request 'precision hint' header that lets the router/scheduler choose a conservative execution mode for requests that prioritize correctness over absolute token throughput.

What this PR does (single-string description): Adds a kernel-compat shim layer in the runtime that detects binary incompatibility or quantization sensitivity for a model+request combination and exposes a low-overhead boolean that the scheduler uses to avoid aggressive fusion/quantization fast-paths; implements an opt-in HTTP header (X-Redwood-Precision-Hint: prefer-correct | prefer-fast | neutral) and SDK flags that map to the same hint; extends continuous-batching logic to respect pe...

title: Add rolling resilience-curve canary with tiered fallback and rate fallthrough
preview: Motivation: We observed slow, multi-hour regressions that did not trigger existing spike-focused detectors; this PR adds a time-weighted 'resilience curve' canary that measures cumulative degradation slope across cohorts and triggers tiered safeguards. High level: 1) rolling resilience curve evaluator that computes ...
text: Motivation: We observed slow, multi-hour regressions that did not trigger existing spike-focused detectors; this PR adds a time-weighted 'resilience curve' canary that measures cumulative degradation slope across cohorts and triggers tiered safeguards. High level: 1) rolling resilience curve evaluator that computes a decay-weighted degradation area under curve (AUC) for latency and error rate across multiple cohort slices (region, client-class, route). 2) temporal quorum voting: requires consensus from multiple independent cohorts before a hard fallback. 3) tiered fallback policy: soft fallback (traffic shaping + alert only), model-variant fallback (route to a lower-cost stable variant), and rate fallthrough (graceful request shedding with progressive SLAs). 4) adaptive probe scheduling that increases probe frequency for cohorts showing rising AUC and backs off when stabilized. Checkl...

title: Triad SDK credential simplifier and stream-flow parity (ergonomics & retry harmonization)
preview: Motivation: SDK consumers across Python/TypeScript/Go reported friction when configuring credential refresh, retry presets, and stream cancellation semantics. This PR introduces a compact, consistent credential surface and harmonizes retry profiles + stream flow semantics across all three SDKs so application code ca...
text:
Motivation: SDK consumers across Python/TypeScript/Go reported friction when configuring credential refresh, retry presets, and stream cancellation semantics. This PR introduces a compact, consistent credential surface and harmonizes retry profiles + stream flow semantics across all three SDKs so application code can be ported with minimal changes.

Summary of changes: consolidated 'CredentialProvider' API shape, introduced a lightweight 'CredHint' for prefetching, aligned retry defaults (idempotent vs. non-idempotent) and added a small stream-control API that exposes cancelable checkpoints for long-running pull-streams. The change is intentionally additive: it builds atop existing adapters and provides compatibility shims for older consumer code.

Per-SDK highlights: Python: new redwood_sdk.cred.CompactProvider and CredHint ctx-manager; retry presets exposed as RetryProfile enum; str...

title: Deferred consensus rollout adapter with heuristic recovery
preview: Motivation: Production rollouts that commit traffic swaps as soon as the control plane reports target replicas healthy still expose end users to transient regressions from cold KV cache, session affinity mismatches, or slow tail latency on a subset of cohorts. This change introduces a 'deferred consensus' adapter th...
text:
Motivation: Production rollouts that commit traffic swaps as soon as the control plane reports target replicas healthy still expose end users to transient regressions from cold KV cache, session affinity mismatches, or slow tail latency on a subset of cohorts. This change introduces a 'deferred consensus' adapter that requires affirmative acknowledgements from a configurable quorum of session-cohorts before finalizing a model swap, plus a heuristic-driven recovery path that can pause, degrade or automatically roll back a swap when post-swap signals indicate regression.

What this PR does (high level): Adds a new rollout adapter that sits in the orchestration plane and coordinates multi-cohort commit semantics: 1) cohort-level readiness probes (application + KV cache warm status + sample latency quantiles) 2) a consensus window where cohorts must report stable metrics for N consecutive...

title: ack-window-coalescing-and-reconnect-probe-for-resumable-streams
preview: Context: We have had intermittent production incidents where client disconnect followed by rapid reconnects created duplicate tool/function calls or left server-side inflight work stranded. This PR introduces a lightweight client ACK window and a reconnect probe handshake to make streaming sessions more resilient to...
text:
Context: We have had intermittent production incidents where client disconnect followed by rapid reconnects created duplicate tool/function calls or left server-side inflight work stranded. This PR introduces a lightweight client ACK window and a reconnect probe handshake to make streaming sessions more resilient to flapping connections while preserving OpenAI compatibility for client-driven cancels.

Goals:
- Allow the client to acknowledge receipt of terminal tokens (finalized structured output / function call invocation) so the server can safely drop inflight state sooner and avoid double-execution on reconnects.
- Coalesce rapid client cancel/soft-abort signals within a short ACK window to reduce noisy cancellation churn during flaky networks.
- Add a reconnect probe (small idempotency-aware handshake) that validates resume tokens and server lease state before resuming expensive w...

title: model-catalog: add filters, compatibility flags, and pricing/limits fields to /models API
preview: Summary: Adds richer filtering and capability metadata to the public model catalog endpoints. This PR introduces query filters for capability-based discovery and model attributes, plus explicit compatibility flags and first-class pricing/limits fields on model descriptors. Motivation: customers have asked for a mach...
text: Summary: Adds richer filtering and capability metadata to the public model catalog endpoints. This PR introduces query filters for capability-based discovery and model attributes, plus explicit compatibility flags and first-class pricing/limits fields on model descriptors. Motivation: customers have asked for a machine-readable way to pick models by capability (e.g., tool-calling, structured output, streaming-stable) and to programmatically surface cost/limit information for routing and billing. The current /models response is sparse and requires ad-hoc parsing of descriptions in downstream services. What this changes: 1) API surface: GET /v1/models now accepts new query params: 'capability' (comma-separated values: chat, completion, embedding, tool_calling, structured_output, streaming), 'openai_compat' (true|false), 'pricing_tier' (free|standard|premium), and 'min_max_tokens' (min,m...
